description_long: This object is a weathered stone grave marker featuring a prominent upright cross mounted on a rectangular pedestal. The cross is of the Latin style, with a slightly rough and aged surface, indicating exposure to the elements over time. The pedestal is blocky and solid, with visible chipping and discoloration, suggesting it is made from granite or a similar durable stone. The entire structure is monochromatic, primarily gray, with patches of lichen or moss adding subtle texture. No visible inscriptions or engravings are present on the surfaces, and the base appears stable and heavy. The object is typical of traditional graveyard monuments found in urban or rural cemeteries.
description: A weathered stone grave marker with a Latin cross atop a rectangular pedestal, showing signs of age and exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full Latin cross and the rectangular pedestal, with the cross arms clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the cross and pedestal, with less detail on the cross arms.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with less pronounced surface features and no visible inscriptions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, highlighting the depth of the pedestal and the vertical shaft of the cross.
